spec.reasoning: The task references absolute cell addresses (J3:L6 and I3:L7), so any row-shifting perturbation (title_row_insert, blank_row_insert) would break the task unless the instruction is rewritten — which is risky. The previous attempt failed exactly because blank rows were inserted at the top, shifting the target rows. Therefore I avoid any row/column positional shifts entirely. Instead I use a distractor column appended after the last column. This adds a plausibly similar column ('Average of Wind Speed (m/s)') that could confuse a model into referencing it, but it does not shift any existing columns or rows. I also set instruction_paraphrase to false per the rules. No column or sheet renames are applied because the instruction explicitly references columns J, K, L by letter, and renaming headers in columns A-F would not affect those references meaningfully — the real surface anchor disruption here comes from the distractor column that looks like it could be relevant energy/generation data.
